prerequisites: An active Microsoft 365 / Azure tenant with access to the SharePoint site you want to replicate.
Permission to register an application in Microsoft Entra ID (Azure Active Directory), or an admin who can grant consent.
A registered Microsoft Entra ID application with the Microsoft Graph Files.Read.All permission granted and admin consent applied.
The list of files to replicate, supplied via the required streams config field (each stream defines a file path/format).
For Service Principal (client credentials) auth: the User Principal Name (UPN) of the account whose drive is accessed.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0
authMethods.summary: Authorize a Microsoft account through the OAuth consent flow. The connector stores credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, credentials.tenant_id and a credentials.refresh_token to obtain Microsoft Graph access tokens on your behalf.

authMethods.name: Service Principal (client credentials)
authMethods.summary: Use application permissions from a Microsoft Entra ID app registration. Supply credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, credentials.tenant_id and credentials.user_principal_name (UPN); no interactive login is required.

setupSteps.title: Register an application in Microsoft Entra ID
setupSteps.body: In the Azure Portal, go to Azure Active Directory > App registrations and choose New registration. Set supported account types to 'Accounts in this organizational directory only'. After creating it, record the Application (Client) ID and the Directory (Tenant) ID; these map to credentials.client_id and credentials.tenant_id.

setupSteps.title: Create a client secret
setupSteps.body: In the app registration, open Certificates & secrets and create a new client secret. Copy the secret value immediately and use it as credentials.client_secret.

setupSteps.title: Grant Microsoft Graph permission
setupSteps.body: Under API permissions, add a permission for Microsoft Graph. For the Service Principal method choose Application permissions and add Files.Read.All, then click Grant admin consent. For OAuth, complete the consent flow which yields credentials.refresh_token.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials and connection settings
setupSteps.body: Configure the required credentials object using either OAuth (client_id, client_secret, tenant_id, refresh_token) or Service Principal (client_id, client_secret, tenant_id, user_principal_name), and define the required streams. Optionally set site_url for a specific SharePoint site, folder_path to limit which folder is searched (leave empty to search all), search_scope (ACCESSIBLE_DRIVES, SHARED_ITEMS, or ALL; default ALL), start_date (YYYY-MM-DD) to bound replication, and delivery_method for raw file transfer.

sources.title: Microsoft SharePoint Source Connector | Airbyte Documentation
